description: Transcription in E. coli is initiated by RNA polymerase holoenzyme (core enzyme + sigma factor) binding to promoter regions. The process is regulated by transcription factors, sigma factor availability, and environmental signals. This fundamental process controls gene expression in response to cellular needs and environmental conditions.

name: Transcription Initiation and Regulation
notes: This model shows the core transcription initiation process. Contains 2 OR logic gates: sigma factor availability and regulatory factor type. E. coli has 7 sigma factors (σ70 is housekeeping). All 25 nodes are uniquely identified and colored according to the 7-color Programming Framework.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified biochemical and structural data from peer-reviewed literature. All molecular mechanisms including open complex formation, sigma factor cycling, and regulatory factor interactions have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents the current scientific consensus on bacterial transcription initiation.
